diagnosis: Lateral medullary (Wallenberg) syndrome from right vertebral/PICA-territory infarct

name: Neurology

name: Neurology
title: Room spins when she rolls over in bed
diagnosis: Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (right posterior semicircular canal)

name: Neurology
title: Spinning that will not stop

name: Neurology
title: Hours of spinning with a roaring, full ear
diagnosis: Ménière's disease (left ear)

name: Neurology
title: Foggy and headachy since Friday's game
diagnosis: Concussion (mild traumatic brain injury)

name: Neurology
title: Growing confusion and a shuffling gait
diagnosis: Chronic subdural hematoma

name: Neurology
title: Knocked out, woke up, now fading fast
diagnosis: Epidural hematoma (middle meningeal artery)

name: Neurology
title: Confused, unsteady, and seeing double

name: Neurology
title: Sudden droopy lid and double vision with a headache

name: Neurology
title: Droopy lid and a dry, sunken eye with arm pain
diagnosis: Horner syndrome due to a Pancoast (superior sulcus) tumor

name: Neurology
title: Fever and worsening back pain with failing legs
diagnosis: Spinal epidural abscess (with T8–T9 vertebral osteomyelitis)

name: Neurology
title: Neck pain and dizziness after a day at the amusement park
diagnosis: Right vertebral artery dissection causing a lateral medullary (Wallenberg) syndrome / posterior-circulation infarct.

name: Neurology
title: A gray curtain over one eye
diagnosis: Amaurosis fugax from artery-to-artery embolism due to symptomatic high-grade right internal carotid artery stenosis.

name: Neurology
title: The room won't stop spinning
diagnosis: Acute cerebellar (right PICA-territory) ischemic infarct, likely cardioembolic from atrial fibrillation.

name: Neurology
title: Weak shoulders and hips after a resuscitation
diagnosis: Bilateral watershed (border-zone) infarcts ('man-in-a-barrel' syndrome) from global hypoperfusion after cardiac arrest.

name: Neurology
title: A first-ever convulsion at work
diagnosis: First unprovoked generalized tonic-clonic seizure with a normal initial workup.

name: Neurology
title: Convulsing and won't stop
diagnosis: Convulsive status epilepticus, precipitated by antiseizure-medication nonadherence.

name: Neurology
title: Daily staring spells at school
diagnosis: Childhood absence epilepsy.

name: Neurology
title: Spells of déjà vu and lip-smacking
diagnosis: Temporal lobe (focal) epilepsy due to right mesial temporal sclerosis.

name: Neurology
title: Morning jerks that fling the toothbrush
diagnosis: Juvenile myoclonic epilepsy.

name: Neurology
title: A brief shaking spell during a fever
diagnosis: Simple febrile seizure.

name: Neurology
title: Prolonged convulsions despite three medications
diagnosis: Psychogenic nonepileptic seizures (functional seizures), confirmed by capture of typical events with normal ictal video-EEG.

name: Neurology
title: Convulsing on the labor ward at 34 weeks
diagnosis: Eclampsia at 34 weeks' gestation (preeclampsia with severe features complicated by generalized seizure).

name: Neurology
title: Shimmering zigzag lights before a pounding headache
diagnosis: Migraine with typical visual aura.

name: Neurology
title: Headaches more days than not
diagnosis: Chronic migraine (≥15 headache days/month with ≥8 migraine days, evolved from episodic migraine), without medication overuse.

name: Neurology
title: Stabbing eye pain that wakes him at 2 a.m.
diagnosis: Episodic cluster headache.

name: Neurology
title: A tight band around her head
diagnosis: Frequent episodic tension-type headache.

name: Neurology
title: New headache with jaw pain and a graying curtain
diagnosis: Giant cell (temporal) arteritis with transient monocular visual loss (impending arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy), with associated polymyalgia rheumatica.

name: Neurology
title: Daily headaches with whooshing in her ears
diagnosis: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension (pseudotumor cerebri), aggravated by weight gain and minocycline use.

name: Neurology
title: Daily headaches despite a purse full of painkillers
diagnosis: Medication overuse headache superimposed on chronic migraine (butalbital combination analgesic and triptan overuse).

name: Neurology
title: Electric shocks in her cheek when she chews
diagnosis: Classical trigeminal neuralgia (right V2/V3) due to neurovascular compression by a superior cerebellar artery loop.

name: Neurology
title: Headache that disappears when she lies down
diagnosis: Spontaneous intracranial hypotension due to a spinal CSF leak

name: Neurology
title: A trembling hand and moving in slow motion
diagnosis: Idiopathic Parkinson's disease

name: Neurology
title: Shaky hands when holding a coffee cup
diagnosis: Essential tremor

name: Neurology
title: Fidgety movements and a changing personality

name: Neurology
title: New stiffness and slowness after starting a stomach medication

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology
title: High fever and rigidity after a medication change

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology
title: Drooping eyelids and double vision by evening
diagnosis: Myasthenia gravis (acetylcholine receptor antibody positive)

name: Neurology

diagnosis: Myasthenic crisis with superimposed cholinergic features, precipitated by infection and a fluoroquinolone

name: Neurology
title: Hand weakness, cramps, and slurred speech

name: Neurology

diagnosis: Bell's palsy (idiopathic peripheral facial nerve palsy)

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology
title: A floppy baby who won't feed

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

name: Neurology
title: Confused and stumbling after a cold

name: Neurology
title: Double vision when looking sideways

name: Neurology
title: Forgetting recent conversations

name: Neurology
title: Memory loss in steps after small strokes

name: Neurology
title: Seeing people who aren't there, with stiffness

name: Neurology

name: Neurology
title: Unsteady gait and new incontinence

name: Neurology

name: Neurology
title: Fever, stiff neck, and a spreading rash

name: Neurology

name: Neurology

title: One-sided hearing loss and ringing

name: Neurology
